Ref: {{ref}}
ESOP Distribution Policy
Rules Governing Participant Distributions
Company
{{companyName}}
Effective Date
{{effectiveDate}}
Approved By
{{approvedBy}}
Reference
{{ref}}
§ 01Purpose & Scope

This Distribution Policy establishes the rules governing distributions from the {{companyName}} Employee Stock Ownership Plan (the “Plan”) to participants. This policy applies to all vested participants who separate from service with the Company, whether through retirement, disability, death, or voluntary or involuntary termination.

This policy governs the timing, method, and form of distributions and is intended to comply with the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (“ERISA”) and the Internal Revenue Code (“IRC”). All distributions are subject to the terms of the Plan document and this policy.

Plan document control: In the event of any conflict between this policy and the Plan document, the Plan document shall govern. This policy is intended as an administrative guide for participants and the Plan Administrator.
§ 02Eligibility

Distributions are available to participants who meet all of the following criteria:

A.Separation from service. The participant has separated from service with the Company due to retirement, disability, death, voluntary termination, or involuntary termination.
B.Vesting. The participant's account is fully vested in accordance with the vesting schedule described in Section 03 below. Unvested portions are forfeited upon separation.
C.Plan year. The participant's account balance has been allocated for the plan year in which separation occurs, subject to the allocation requirements in the Plan document.
Note: Hardship distributions are not permitted from the ESOP. Participants experiencing financial hardship should consult with a tax advisor regarding other available options.
§ 03Vesting Schedule

Participants vest in their ESOP accounts according to the following schedule:

{{vestingType}}
{{cliffPeriod}}
{{gradedSchedule}}
{{fullVestingYears}} years of service
{{acceleratedVestingTrigger}}
Important: Participants who separate before becoming fully vested will forfeit the unvested portion of their account. Forfeitures are reallocated to remaining participants in accordance with the Plan document.
§ 04Distribution Timing

Distributions are made in accordance with the following timing rules:

A.Waiting period. Distributions begin no earlier than {{distributionWaitingPeriod}} after the date of separation from service, as required by the Plan document.
B.Installment option. Participants may elect to receive distributions in substantially equal annual installments over a period of up to {{maxInstallmentYears}} years.
C.Lump sum option. Participants may elect to receive their entire vested account balance in a single lump sum distribution.
D.Distribution window. Distributions are processed annually during the month of {{distributionWindowMonth}}. Requests received after the processing deadline will be included in the next annual distribution.
E.Required minimum distributions. Participants who reach age 73 (or such other age as required by law) must begin receiving required minimum distributions regardless of separation status.
§ 05Distribution Methods

The Plan provides the following distribution methods:

A.Cash distributions. The Company purchases shares from the ESOP trust at the current fair market value, and the trust distributes cash to the participant. This is the standard distribution method for all participants.
B.Installment payments. Participants who elect the installment option receive annual cash payments calculated by dividing the vested account balance by the number of remaining installments, adjusted for investment performance.
C.In-kind stock distribution. Not available. As a privately held company, all distributions are made in cash.
Note: The Company's repurchase obligation to fund cash distributions is a material financial commitment. The Company maintains a repurchase obligation reserve to ensure sufficient liquidity to meet distribution obligations.
§ 06Diversification Rights

Participants who meet the eligibility requirements below may elect to diversify a portion of their ESOP account in accordance with ERISA §404(c):

10 years of plan participation AND age 55 or older
Up to 25% of account balance may be diversified
Up to 50% of account balance may be diversified
90 days following delivery of annual account statement
{{diversificationOptions}}

Diversification elections are irrevocable for the applicable plan year. Participants who do not make an election within the 90-day window forfeit diversification rights for that year. Diversified amounts remain in the Plan but are invested in the selected alternatives.

§ 07Valuation of Shares

The fair market value (“FMV”) of Company stock held in the ESOP is determined as follows:

A.Annual appraisal. FMV is determined annually by an independent, qualified appraiser selected by the Plan Trustee. The appraisal is conducted in accordance with IRS Revenue Ruling 59-60 and applicable ERISA requirements.
B.Distribution value. The value used for distributions is based on the most recent annual appraisal completed prior to the distribution date.
C.Interim valuation. An interim valuation may be obtained for distributions exceeding {{interimValuationThreshold}} at the discretion of the Plan Trustee.
D.Statement delivery. Participants receive an annual account statement within {{statementDeliveryDays}} days of the annual valuation date, reflecting their vested account balance and diversification rights.
§ 08Tax Implications

Participants should be aware of the following tax considerations:

A.Ordinary income. ESOP distributions are taxed as ordinary income in the year received, regardless of whether received as a lump sum or in installments.
B.Early withdrawal penalty. An additional 10% tax applies to distributions made before age 59½, unless an exception applies (separation from service after age 55, disability, or substantially equal periodic payments).
C.Rollover options. Participants may roll over eligible distributions to an Individual Retirement Account (“IRA”) or another qualified plan on a tax-deferred basis. Direct rollover elections must be made on the distribution election form.
D.§1042 rollover. The selling shareholder who elected §1042 treatment at the time of the ESOP transaction should consult with their tax advisor regarding any implications for distributions from the Plan.
E.Withholding. Federal income tax withholding of 20% is mandatory on eligible rollover distributions not directly rolled over. State withholding may also apply.
Tax advice: Forhemit does not provide tax advice. Participants should consult with a qualified tax professional regarding their individual circumstances.
§ 09Administrative Procedures

To request a distribution, participants must complete the following steps:

A.Distribution request. The participant submits a completed Distribution Request Form to the Plan Administrator. Forms are available from the Plan Administrator or the Company's HR department.
B.Rollover election. If applicable, the participant completes a Direct Rollover Election Form specifying the receiving institution and account.
C.Tax withholding election. The participant completes a Tax Withholding Election Form. Federal withholding is mandatory at 20% for eligible rollover distributions not directly rolled over.
D.Processing time. Distribution requests are processed within {{processingDays}} business days of receipt of all required documentation.
E.Payment method. Distributions are made by check mailed to the participant's address on file or by direct deposit to the participant's designated bank account.
Questions: For questions regarding distributions, contact the Plan Administrator at {{planAdministratorEmail}} or {{planAdministratorPhone}}.
—Plan Administrator / Trustee Approval
